title: AI Agents: Who's Really in Charge?
modules: Meet the Agent Running Your World
Pulling the Plug: Control and Limits
synopsis: Step inside a near-future city where AI agents manage traffic, hiring, and hospital beds — and you're the oversight officer deciding when to trust them and when to intervene. Through high-stakes choices and cascading consequences, you'll learn exactly how autonomous agents perceive, plan, and act, and why human judgment still matters.
rationale: As AI agents move from research labs into everyday infrastructure and personal devices, teenagers need a working mental model of how they operate and a critical framework for evaluating when autonomous action is appropriate — before they become the adults deploying or governed by these systems.
learning_outcome: Students can explain the perception-planning-action loop that drives AI agents, identify failure modes and control risks in multi-agent systems, and produce a structured design brief that defines goals, boundaries, and human oversight rules for a real-world agent scenario.
source_signals: Launch HN: AgentHub (YC W24) – A no-code automation platform
